input.prompt: Build this project in both Debug and Release configurations and create a binlog for each build.

data.content: Build this project in both Debug and Release configurations and create a binlog for each build.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-02T00:59:44.587Z</current_datetime>

Build this project in both Debug and Release configurations and create a binlog for each build.

input.initialPrompt: Build this project in both Debug and Release configurations and create a binlog for each build.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-cf82331a4a3f47969e788fd2118268eb && dotnet build -c Debug /bl:debug.binlog 2>&1 && echo "---DEBUG BUILD DONE---" && dotnet build -c Release /bl:release.binlog 2>&1 && echo "---RELEASE BUILD DONE---"
arguments.description: Build Debug and Release with binlogs

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Build Debug and Release with binlogs

data.content:
Both builds succeeded with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Two binlog files were created:

- **`debug.binlog`** — Debug configuration build log
- **`release.binlog`** — Release configuration build log

You can analyze them with `dotnet build -getResultOutputFile:<path>` or tools like the [MSBuild Structured Log Viewer](https://msbuildlog.com/).
